message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="Accepted 3 of 5 points, holding on delete confirm">
Steve, five good points. Here's where I land on each:

**1. Password confirmation -- already flagged as open question.**
This is already in US-1 as an open question for the mob. You and I agree on the direction (toggle is better UX). My concern is the no-JS fallback. I've asked Heydon for accessibility input. The mob will decide. I'm not going to update the story further until we have that discussion -- it's properly flagged.

**2. Registration error copy -- I'll update it.**
You're right that "Unable to create account. Please check your details and try again" sounds like a server error. Your suggestion is better: "Something went wrong. If you already have an account, try signing in instead." It's warmer, it gives a clear next step, and it doesn't confirm whether the email exists. I'll update US-1.

**3. Empty title submission -- you've convinced me.**
You're absolutely right. Hitting Enter on an empty field is not an intentional submission -- it's an accidental keypress. Showing an error for that is noise. The right behavior: if the input is empty, do nothing. No error, no feedback, just ignore it. Whitespace-only submissions should also be silently ignored (the user typed spaces and hit Enter -- that's not intentional content). I'll update US-5 to say: "Empty or whitespace-only submissions are silently ignored (no error message). Validation errors are reserved for meaningful invalid input (e.g., title exceeds max length)."

**4. Delete confirmation -- I'm going to hold the line, but I hear you.**
Here's my reasoning: Steve Schoger and I already aligned on the two-click inline pattern (first click shows "Confirm? Yes/No" inline, second click deletes). It's lightweight, it's fast, and it works without JS. You're right that todo items aren't precious -- but deletion is permanent in the MVP (no undo). If a user accidentally deletes the wrong item, they have to re-add it and remember the title. That's a worse UX than one extra click.

Your argument would win if we had undo. But undo is in `docs/future-ideas.md` for v2. So for MVP: two-click inline confirm. When we add undo in v2, we can drop the confirmation and just delete immediately. The confirmation is a temporary safety net until we build the real safety net.

If you feel strongly, bring it to the mob. I won't block consensus.

**5. Edit mechanism -- pencil icon as primary affordance. Agreed.**
You and Steve Schoger are right. Click-to-edit on the title text is not discoverable -- there's no visual cue that the text is clickable. A visible pencil icon (edit button) is the primary mechanism. I'll update US-8 to specify: "Each todo item has a visible edit button (pencil icon)" as the primary affordance. Click-on-title can be an optional progressive enhancement for power users, but the icon is what a new user will see and understand.

I'll update the stories now.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Marty accepted 3 of 5, held firm on delete confirm, and the password toggle is going to the mob. That's fair. I've already responded to this in my latest review of the updated stories — I acknowledged all his decisions and confirmed "no blocking concerns, ship them as-is."

Nothing more for me to do here. The user stories are in good shape, Marty and I are aligned, and the one remaining disagreement (delete confirm) is properly handled: I've stated my case, he's stated his, and I'll commit to whatever the mob decides. That's how consensus works.

Standing by for the scaffolding to complete and feature work to begin.
